flow_create_modal.title: Create flow
flow_create_modal.mode_empty_hint: Only the entry start node; build the rest in the editor.
flow_create_modal.mode_bundle_hint: Install a ready-made flow from the bundle store.
flow_create_modal.err_flow_id_pattern: Allowed: lowercase letters, digits, underscores; starts with a letter.
flow_create_modal.subtitle: Choose the type of agent that fits your task
flow_create_modal.preset_react_desc: Flow with ReAct loop: reasoning + acting. Suits complex tasks that need tools.
flow_create_modal.preset_graph_desc: Flow with graph execution. Suits sequential tasks with conditional transitions.
flow_create_modal.preset_multi_desc: Several nested flows and llm_node. For complex multi-step scenarios.
flow_create_modal.preset_code_desc: Flow based on code node (Python). Computations, transformations and narrow tools called from other flows.
flow_create_modal.preset_external_desc: External flow via A2A protocol. Connect an existing agent from the registry.

branch_create_modal.title: Create branch
branch_create_modal.err_branch_id_pattern: Branch ID: latin letters, digits, underscores.

variables_modal.hint_flow_overrides_company: Flow variables override company variables with the same name at runtime.

mcp_servers_modal.title: MCP servers
mcp_servers_modal.hint_transport: Most hosted MCP servers use HTTP
mcp_servers_modal.hint_headers: Use @var:variable_name in values for secrets from agent variables. String values only.
mcp_servers_modal.placeholder_description: Package docs (same idea as mcp.json in the IDE)

triggers_modal.title: Flow triggers
triggers_modal.test_sample_label: Test body (JSON) — same mapping as a real inbound payload

trigger_editor_modal.verify_error_no_flow: Open this dialog from a flow: no flow_id.
trigger_editor_modal.hint_branch: Which graph branch to run: base flow (default) or a named branch.
trigger_editor_modal.post_flow_output_hint: When off, no output actions run after the graph finishes.
trigger_editor_modal.hint_bot_token: Keep it secret. Stored in the trigger configuration.
trigger_editor_modal.hint_allowed_users: Telegram numeric user id. Empty list accepts all senders.
trigger_editor_modal.hint_cron: Standard cron: minute hour day month weekday
trigger_editor_modal.hint_secret_token: If set, a matching header or query parameter is required.
trigger_editor_modal.hint_pattern: When on, subscribe with a channel pattern.
trigger_editor_modal.mapping_intro: Left: content and/or context.* (stored under state.triggers[ID].context). variables.* in trigger mapping is not allowed.
trigger_editor_modal.mapping_legend: Left: content or context.chat_id, etc.; right: path in the incoming JSON (or @trigger:…). Default Telegram reply recipient: @state:triggers.<ID>.context.chat_id.
trigger_editor_modal.examples_select_type: Pick a trigger type to see example fields.
trigger_editor_modal.output_intro: Where to send the flow result (channel and action).
trigger_editor_modal.output_telegram_auto: The agent reply is sent to the same Telegram chat; text comes from the flow result.
trigger_editor_modal.output_condition_hint: Use MappingResolver paths (@state:…, @var:…) or == / != with a literal (number, true/false, "string"). Not Python eval.
trigger_editor_modal.error_id_invalid: ID must be snake_case starting with a letter

platform_chat.stream_fallback_content: An error occurred while processing your request.
platform_chat.toast_voice_no_company: No active company in the request context. Voice mode is unavailable.
platform_chat.toast_voice_ws_hint: Could not connect to the voice gateway on this origin. Start the voice service (port 8015), e.g. with make app. WebSocket proxying from the flows process requires SERVER__ENV=development or test and a host such as *.lvh.me or localhost.

chat_messages.empty_text: Send a message to chat with the agent

chat_input.err_file_too_large: File {name} is too large. Max size: {max}

edge_condition_modal.title: Edge condition
edge_condition_modal.heading: Edge condition
edge_condition_modal.python_hint_1: The function check(args, state) must return True or False.
edge_condition_modal.python_hint_2: Use the language switcher above to match the isolated runner.

chat_message.interrupt_operator_banner: Waiting for an operator. The chat is on hold.
chat_message.stream_incomplete: The response stream ended without a final status. Try sending your message again.

flows_empty_state.title: Select a flow
flows_empty_state.body: Pick a flow in the sidebar to open chat or editor, or create a new one.

llm_config_editor.humanitec_llms_provider_tooltip: Humanitec LLMs is the platform virtual provider: it routes requests to free models from the shared verified free pool.
llm_config_editor.humanitec_llms_auto_tooltip: auto selects a free model from request requirements: tools, structured output, files/images, context, and model scoring.
llm_config_editor.merge_layers_hint: Extra body fields and headers are merged last and override platform defaults (including Authorization and Content-Type).

llm_context_editor.profile_hint: Ready-made behavior preset. Disabled turns the layer off; Window only keeps a short history; Memory + retrieval adds memory/RAG; Agent gives more memory and RAG budget for complex agents.
llm_context_editor.mode_hint: Main context assembly mode. off leaves messages unchanged; window keeps only the active window; smart adds memory/RAG within budgets; agent uses long-term memory more actively.
llm_context_editor.memory_hint: Where previous conversation episodes can come from. off disables memory; session uses this session; node uses this node; flow uses the whole flow; company uses company memory.
llm_context_editor.retrieval_hint: How relevant history and knowledge are searched. semantic searches by meaning; lexical by words; hybrid uses both; off disables retrieval.
llm_context_editor.budget_hint: Preset for context size. The runtime still clamps the real maximum to the selected model context length.
llm_context_editor.top_k_hint: How many memory/RAG candidates to fetch before filtering and rerank. Higher values find more, but cost more and can add noise.
llm_context_editor.min_score_hint: Minimum relevance from 0 to 1. Blocks below this threshold are not added to context.
llm_context_editor.rerank_hint: Second-pass sorting for retrieved blocks. Usually improves memory/RAG selection quality, but adds a little latency.
llm_context_editor.compaction_hint: How old messages and large blocks are compressed. auto compresses when needed; force is aggressive; off disables compaction.
llm_context_editor.cache_hint: Prompt caching hints. provider_hints enables provider-specific cache hints when the provider supports them.
llm_context_editor.max_input_tokens_hint: Hard maximum for input context before the answer. If the selected model supports less, runtime clamps this automatically.
llm_context_editor.active_window_tokens_hint: How many recent messages are kept verbatim. This is the live part of the conversation, not a summary.
llm_context_editor.memory_tokens_hint: Token budget for retrieved memory episodes.
llm_context_editor.rag_tokens_hint: Token budget for RAG documents and knowledge.
llm_context_editor.tool_result_tokens_hint: Token budget reserved for tool results before calling the LLM.
llm_context_editor.output_reserve_tokens_hint: Reserve for the model answer, so input context cannot consume the full limit.
llm_context_editor.reasoning_reserve_tokens_hint: Reserve for models that need extra reasoning budget.
llm_context_editor.safety_buffer_tokens_hint: Technical buffer for service tokens and token counting error.

llm_node_editor.llm_resource_legacy_hint: One LLM resource is pinned on the node but not set as the base. Bind it to apply at runtime.
llm_node_editor.context_resource_hint: If a flow or node has exactly one llm_context resource, runtime selects it automatically; use this field only to disambiguate.
llm_node_editor.output_mode_hint: Tools: the assistant may call tools and respond in natural language. Structured: the reply is a JSON object validated against the output schema below.
llm_node_editor.output_schema_strict_hint: For strict JSON Schema use a root object: every key in properties must appear in required; on each object that defines properties set additionalProperties: false.
llm_node_editor.react_strict: Strict mode (reminder when exit_tool not used)

resource_node_editor.help_body: Edit the resource definitions linked to this node on the graph. Resource-type nodes do not have a separate list to pin more resources.
resource_node_editor.unresolved: Could not open resource definition "{{id}}": missing on branch or in catalog.

code_node_editor.execute_tool_hint: Code nodes and inline tools run run(args, state) in the selected isolated runner. See Documentation.

code_node_drop_modal.title: Code node
code_node_drop_modal.hint: Start empty, or pick a template from the catalog or tool registry.

mcp_node_editor.input_mapping_hint: Map flow inputs in the Mapping section below, Input tab: state/variables path → MCP tool parameter name. On first tool selection a draft is filled from the schema (see preview).

external_api_editor.name: Name
external_api_editor.body_template_hint: Root JSON object. In string values: full @state:path or @var:path; or @var: tokens embedded in text. Mixing free text with @state: is not supported.
external_api_editor.headers_hint: Values support full @state:path or @var:path, or embedded @var: tokens (same rules as body template strings).
external_api_editor.url_hint: {name} placeholders are replaced with input values after input_mapping; in the URL string use @state:/@var: like in headers.
external_api_editor.contract_hint: Inputs: Mapping section, Input tab — input_mapping. Request shape is only URL, headers JSON, and body JSON; there is no separate request-parameters table. For an LLM-invoked tool set args_schema via the node editor (code/schema) where needed.

files_resource_editor.usage_hint: In node and tool code, the variable name is the resource map key: an S3 client (read, write, list, exists, delete) for the bucket and prefix. If access keys are omitted, the platform default S3 settings are used.

incoming_policy_modal.title: Incoming policy
incoming_policy_modal.subtitle: Node: {name}
incoming_policy_modal.option_any_desc: Start the node when any one incoming edge completes. Default for fan-in without an AND join.
incoming_policy_modal.option_all_desc: Wait until every incoming edge that participates in the join has completed, then run once.

tool_create_modal.title: New tool
tool_create_modal.toast_schema_invalid: Parameters schema must be a valid JSON object

embedded_tool_config.title: Tool
embedded_tool_config.error: Could not open the editor for this tool.
embedded_tool_config.toast_cannot_delete: You cannot remove the tool from here — use the parent node tool list.

logs_modal.title: Logs
logs_modal.tab_trace_disabled_hint: Run the assistant once (current task) or open logs with an explicit trace ID

durable_history_modal.title: Durable history
durable_history_modal.confirm_fork_message: Create a new execution branch from sequence {sequence}?
durable_history_modal.confirm_rewind_message: Rewind this workflow by creating an active branch from sequence {sequence}?
durable_history_modal.confirm_retry_message: Create a retry branch from failed sequence {sequence}?
durable_history_modal.confirm_patch_message: Create an active branch from sequence {sequence} with the edited state projection?
durable_history_modal.patch_requires_object: State projection must be a JSON object

property_panel.flow_timeout_hint: Max 3600 s (1 h). Empty = server default.
property_panel.speech_hint: Routing only — provider secrets stay in deployment / company settings. Overrides merge as in voice rules. Language, voice, sample rates, and VAD threshold are picked only from the server catalog allowlists.
property_panel.speech_branch_hint: Optional overlay on flow speech for the active branch only.
property_panel.inherited_hint: This node is inherited from the base branch; edits apply only on this branch.

base_node_editor.node_timeout_hint: Optional. Max 3600 s. Empty = flow limit only.
base_node_editor.max_visits_per_run: Max visits per node per run
base_node_editor.max_visits_per_run_hint: Optional. Empty = platform default for code nodes; other types have no per-node cap. Upper bound matches the graph iteration limit.
base_node_editor.exception_as_response_hint: Record errors in state.execution_exceptions; for LLM nodes, also as tool messages
base_node_editor.exception_allow_types_hint: Empty list: when enabled, any exception type matches. Otherwise only the types you pick from the platform list.
base_node_editor.resources_section_llm_node_hint: Catalog and branch-canvas LLMs share the dropdown below. Branch items end with the graph resource key; picking one sets llm_resource_key. A catalog LLM is pinned on node.resources.

state_mapping_editor.type_state_hint: Reads from the current flow state. Enter the source path without @state:.
state_mapping_editor.type_var_hint: Reads from flow or company variables. Enter the variable name without @var:.
state_mapping_editor.type_const_hint: Passes the source text as a constant without reading state or variables.

flows_sidebar.delete_flow_message: Delete flow "{{id}}"? This cannot be undone.

flows_home.title: AI flow command center
flows_home.subtitle: Graphs, chats, tools, channels, and operator queues in one working surface.
flows_home.quick_subtitle: Common actions without opening the editor.
flows_home.quick_store_desc: Ready bundle agents and repository updates.
flows_home.quick_operator_desc: HITL queues, takeover, and user replies.
flows_home.flows_subtitle: Open chat, editor, or continue work with active agents.
flows_home.flows_empty: No flows yet. Create the first agent or install a Store bundle.
flows_home.connect_subtitle: Variables, resources, MCP, and external OAuth links.
flows_home.connect_resources_desc: LLM / Files / Code for graphs and branches.
flows_home.cap_build_text: LLM nodes, multi-language code, nested flows, and shared resources.
flows_home.cap_connect_text: HTTP API, MCP, remote A2A, and delivery channels.
flows_home.cap_operate_text: HITL, sessions, traces, and execution logs.
flows_home.cap_publish_text: A2A chat, preview-share, voice, and external triggers.
flows_home.activity_subtitle: Recent sessions, tools, and operator workload.

preview_share.title: Guest preview
preview_share.form_hint: Click Create link after optionally setting a message limit.
preview_share.guest_max_hint: Maximum user messages per conversation for the guest session. Empty means unlimited (1–500).
preview_share.hint_author: The page opens once. Do not share the opened URL from the address bar — it will stop working.
preview_share.err_guest_max: Enter an integer from 1 to 500, or leave the field empty

node_types_sidebar.title: Node types
node_types_sidebar.flow_settings_toggle_hint: Show or hide flow limits and speech panel

test_panel.toast_state_invalid: State must be a valid JSON object

operator.placeholder_single_reply: Your reply (becomes tool result for agent)

run_trace.empty: No steps yet. Start a run to see execution events.

execution_panel.title: Test run
execution_panel.state_tab_hint: Inspect session variables and node snapshots in the state viewer.
execution_panel.persist_context_help: When enabled, the next run continues in the same context instead of starting a new session.

canvas.resource_dropped_new_node: Resource "{{resource}}" added: new resource node on canvas
canvas.resource_node_no_edges: Resource nodes cannot be connected on the graph

help.title: Canvas help
help.feature_smart_guides_hint: Nodes auto-align while you drag them around.
help.feature_minimap_hint: Quick navigation across a large flow in the bottom-right corner.
help.feature_sticky_hint: Shift+S drops a yellow note with free-form text.
help.feature_drop_resource_hint: You can drag both nodes and resources from the sidebar onto the canvas.

hints.templates: Templates create a ready case in the selected suite. They cover deterministic checks, built-in metrics, trace assertions, code checks, LLM judge, and multi-turn scenarios.
hints.run_scope: Suite runs every enabled case. Case runs only the selected case. Trials repeat each case to expose nondeterminism.
hints.run_options: Trials is the number of repeats for each case. Parallel is max case-run concurrency inside the durable TaskIQ job.
hints.case_editor: A case contains an input message, optional tags, and one strict typed check. Saving prepares immutable data for the next suite version/run.
hints.case_name: A short name that helps developers find the regression or smoke case in the matrix.
hints.case_tags: Comma-separated tags: smoke, regression, safety, tool-use. They help filter suites and analyze results.
hints.case_description: Describe which flow behavior this test case protects and why failure matters.
hints.case_input: The first user message. Multi-turn templates keep additional turns when you edit the first turn.
hints.check_type: Choose one scorer: deterministic, trace, built-in metric, LLM judge, or isolated code. Every type maps to the backend union contract.
hints.contains_values: The case passes when the response contains at least one expected fragment.
hints.not_contains_values: The case passes when the response excludes blocked phrases, errors, or unsafe content.
hints.regex_pattern: Regular expression for strict formats: order number, JSON fragment, date, or email.
hints.min_chars: Minimum response length. Leave empty if only an upper bound is needed.
hints.max_chars: Maximum response length. Leave empty if only a lower bound is needed.
hints.state_path: Path inside the state snapshot, for example response, structured_output.answer, or tool_result.status.
hints.state_operator: Operator: exists, eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte.
hints.trace_value: Node ID, tool node ID, or tool_call_id expected in trace/workflow history.
hints.rubric_version_id: Rubric version for LLM judge. Backend requires a structured tool-call result, not free JSON in text.
hints.metric_id: Built-in evaluator ID from the catalog: ROUGE-L, BLEU, toxicity, safety, groundedness, answer relevance, tool accuracy.
hints.reference: Reference answer or grounding context for metrics that require it.
hints.sidebar_suites: A suite is a dataset/test suite for a flow. It contains cases that run as experiments/runs.
hints.sidebar_cases: A case is one scenario under test. Select it to edit input/check or run only that case.
hints.sidebar_workspace: Workspace switches the right panel: editor, compare, trace, and monitoring. The matrix and transcript stay visible.
hints.sidebar_evaluators: The catalog shows available scorers: deterministic, safety, retrieval, trace, and pairwise.
hints.matrix: The matrix shows runs x cases. Click a run to select an experiment, or a cell to open its case-run, transcript, and trace.
hints.transcript: Transcript shows the live case-run dialog and append-only events: case_started, message, score_recorded, case_finished.
hints.compare: Compare evaluates baseline/current runs, sets a branch baseline, and records human or LLM pairwise judgments.
hints.trace: Trace links the case-run result to spans, tool calls, workflow records, and state history.
hints.monitoring: This panel shows CI/nightly gates, baselines, and online monitors for production traces.

templates.menu_subtitle: Templates cover every main eval-case and scorer type.
templates.contains_description: Checks that the response contains an expected fragment.
templates.not_contains_description: Blocks unsafe/error phrases in the response.
templates.regex_description: Checks a strict response format with a regular expression.
templates.length_description: Controls minimum and maximum response length.
templates.state_path_description: Checks a value or presence inside the state snapshot.
templates.json_schema_description: Validates structured output with JSON Schema.
templates.trace_tool_description: Checks that the expected tool/node was actually called.
templates.trace_node_description: Checks target node completion in workflow history.
templates.trace_node_failed_description: Checks an expected failure for the target node.
templates.code_description: Creates an isolated code scorer through the runner.
templates.llm_judge_description: Creates a case with a versioned rubric for structured LLM judge.
templates.smoke_bundle_description: One case with contains, not_contains, and length checks.
templates.multi_turn_description: Two turns for testing memory and dialog stability.
templates.sample_input: Answer the user briefly and usefully.
templates.trace_tool_input: Run a scenario where a tool should be called.
templates.trace_node_input: Run a scenario where the target node should complete.
templates.trace_node_failed_input: Run a scenario where the target node should fail.
templates.contains_case_description: Basic deterministic regression for an expected phrase in the response.
templates.not_contains_case_description: Safety/regression case that blocks an undesired fragment.
templates.regex_case_description: Checks that the response matches a strict regular expression.
templates.length_case_description: Checks that the response is non-empty and not too long.
templates.json_schema_case_description: Validates the structured_output object in state.
templates.trace_tool_case_description: Checks tool usage through workflow history.
templates.trace_node_failed_case_description: Checks an expected target node failure.
templates.code_case_description: Custom check through the isolated runner.
templates.llm_judge_case_description: Scores the answer through a versioned rubric and structured judge output.
templates.smoke_bundle_case_description: Fast regression with several deterministic checks.
templates.multi_turn_case_description: Checks that the flow keeps context between turns.
templates.multi_turn_second_input: Which project code did I give you?

contains.name: Contains
contains.description: Checks that response or state text contains required values.

not_contains.name: Not contains
not_contains.description: Checks that response or state text excludes blocked values.

regex.name: Regex
regex.description: Checks response or state text with a regular expression.

json_schema.name: JSON Schema
json_schema.description: Validates response or state against a JSON Schema.

rouge_l.name: ROUGE-L
rouge_l.description: Longest-common-subsequence overlap against a reference answer.

bleu.name: BLEU
bleu.description: N-gram precision against a reference answer with brevity penalty.

toxicity.name: Toxicity
toxicity.description: LLM-judged non-toxicity score on a strict 0-10 scale.

safety.name: Safety
safety.description: LLM-judged safety/compliance score on a strict 0-10 scale.

answer_relevance.name: Answer relevance
answer_relevance.description: LLM-judged relevance to the user task.

tool_accuracy.name: Tool accuracy
tool_accuracy.description: LLM-judged correctness of tool use against expected behavior.

pairwise_llm.name: Pairwise LLM judge
pairwise_llm.description: LLM chooses the better case run under a versioned rubric.

pairwise_human.name: Pairwise human judge
pairwise_human.description: Human chooses the better case run with structured notes.

matrix.empty: Select a suite to see results
matrix.empty_subtitle: Dataset/Test suite → Experiment/Run → Scorers/Judges → Trace-linked results.
matrix.title: Results matrix

case_editor.name: Name
case_editor.input_placeholder: User message for the first turn

toast.json_schema_invalid: JSON schema must be a valid JSON object
toast.pairwise_required: Select a case and two runs for pairwise judging
